The Trump administration is seriously considering an additional deployment of 10,000 US ground troops to the Middle East, the Wall Street Journal reported. 

The US has also deployed uncrewed drone boats in Gulf waters for surveillance and interdiction, the first significant operational use of autonomous maritime systems in a live conflict. Trump paused airstrikes on Iranian energy infrastructure and extended the negotiating window into April. 

The same administration is simultaneously building up its ground presence at scale. Military planners are preparing for scenarios well beyond aerial operations. A diplomatic pause and a ground force buildup are not contradictory, unless you believe the negotiations are genuine.

The US is talking negotiations with Iran while simultaneously planning a 10,000-troop ground deployment. Is this military pressure to strengthen the diplomatic hand or preparation for a ground campaign regardless of how talks go?

>> PCB Material Shortages Intensify as CCL Lead Times Hit 6 Months, Quota System Imposed

- The surge in AI applications is accelerating PCB material upgrades, deepening raw material supply shortages.

- Notably, beyond sustained price increases, some IC substrate manufacturers have flagged that CCL supply has been disrupted for several months due to capacity constraints in fiberglass fabric and copper foil, with product lead times stretching up to six months — prompting affected suppliers to implement quota systems.

- As upstream material shortages have recurred multiple times, PCB manufacturers now find themselves unable to cope with supply constraints through inventory buildup alone.

- Instead, they are coordinating order volumes with upstream suppliers based on actual consumption in order to optimally fulfill customer demand.

- This situation signals that rising demand for high-end products such as IC substrates, HDI, and high-layer-count (HLC) boards is intensifying material competition within the PCB industry.

- To prevent customer over-ordering and duplicate orders, CCL suppliers are increasingly responding with allocation policies.

- Meanwhile, PCB manufacturers are adopting a two-pronged risk mitigation strategy: modest product price increases to reflect rising costs of fiberglass fabric and copper foil, alongside pre-confirmed order volumes with customers — building a structure that secures sufficient material supply from CCL suppliers up to six months ahead of mass production.

- Industry participants note that shortages of T-glass fiberglass fabric used in IC substrates are particularly acute, with the supply crunch expected to persist through 2026 — with resolution contingent on Nitto Boseki's capacity expansion timeline. As top-tier suppliers concentrate on high-end product lines, shortages of mid-to-low-grade E-glass fiberglass fabric have also begun to emerge.

- CCL suppliers further highlight that copper foil prices continue to climb in tandem with base copper metal prices — and that these price increases are not one-time adjustments, but rather stepped hikes expected to rise in double-digit percentages with each successive round.

It builds a real semantic map of your entire codebase before your AI touches a single line of code. 17 tools. 43 file extensions. Actual syntax trees, not regex.

Here's what it does that nothing else does:

→ Tree-sitter AST parsing across 43 file extensions. Real syntax trees with function signatures, class methods, and symbol ranges
→ Spectral Clustering that groups semantically related files into labeled clusters
→ Obsidian-style wikilinks that map high-level features to their corresponding code files
→ Blast radius tracing. Before any change, it shows every file and line where a symbol is imported or used
→ Semantic search by meaning. Ask what something does, not what it's called. Uses Ollama vector embeddings with disk cache
→ Identifier-level retrieval for functions, classes, and variables with ranked call sites and line numbers
→ In-memory property graph with decay scoring that tracks how your codebase actually connects

Here's the part that changes how AI writes code:

The propose_commit tool. It validates changes against strict rules, creates a shadow restore point, and only then writes to disk. If something goes wrong, one command undoes any AI change without touching your git history.

Your AI can't just freestyle your production code anymore.

Works with Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, and OpenCode.

Powered by Ollama locally. No external API calls needed for embeddings.

100% Open Source. MIT License.

(Link in the comments)

IRAN:

US President Trump said, "As per Iranian Government request, please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time. Talks are ongoing and, despite erroneous statements to the contrary by the Fake News Media, and others, they are going very well." (Truth Social)

Iran hasn’t requested a 10-day pause on strikes on its energy plants and is yet to deliver a final response to a 15-point plan to end the war, peace talk mediators said to the WSJ. Iranian officials have told the mediators that they are interested in negotiations but the country’s leadership is yet to weigh in and give a final decision. (WSJ)

The Pentagon is looking at sending up to 10,000 additional ground troops to the Middle East to give President Trump more military options even as he weighs peace talks with Tehran, Department of Defense officials said to the WSJ. (WSJ)

CNN sources echo other recent source reports (Axios) that Trump is weighing several options for dramatically escalating the war against Iran should his latest push for diplomacy fail. (CNN)

Two large container ships linked to China’s state-owned Cosco Shipping Corp. attempted to exit the Persian Gulf on Friday through the Strait of Hormuz, before making an abrupt U-turn near Iran and motoring back. (BBG)

Iran's IRGC Navy said three container ships of different nationalities started to move towards the corridor on Friday, saying they were turned back with a warning from the IRGC Navy. (Newsquawk)

FT sources report the Israeli military is increasingly sceptical that regime change in Iran will be possible in the coming weeks, casting doubt on one of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s core war aims as the Islamic republic shows its ability to endure intense bombardment. (FT)

FT reports that some third-country shipping vessels were reflagging as Pakistani to pass through the Strait. “Lots of shipping lines are changing flags to be sailing under Pakistan registration,” said a source. Another source said the arrangements were intended as “an olive branch to Trump”. (FT)

The United Arab Emirates has told allies that it would participate in a multinational maritime task force intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as it lobbies to form a coalition to ensure shipping is able to pass through the vital Gulf waterway. (FT)

SMIC, China's largest chipmaker, has sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, two senior Trump administration officials said to Reuters, raising questions about Beijing's stance in the month-old U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran. (RTRS)

Iran has allowed Malaysian vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf to return home through the Strait of Hormuz, the Southeast Asian nation’s prime minister said in a televised address on Thursday evening. (BBG)

'U.S. and Israel Have Pounded—but Not Eliminated—Iran’s Missile Threat' WSJ article notes Iran has shifted to firing from deeper inside its territory with longer-range missiles, and less frequently, after airstrikes early in the war inflicted a heavy toll on Iranian bases and truck launchers near the Persian Gulf coastline. (WSJ)

US battle damage and replacement of losses over the first three weeks of the war likely costs roughly $1.4 billion to $2.9 billion, according to Elaine McCusker, a top Pentagon budget official during the first Trump administration who has been tracking the cost of the conflict for the American Enterprise Institute. (WSJ) 

Ships have been forgoing cargo in order to take shipping fuel to critical ports, as shortages caused by strikes in the Gulf pile almost $5bn of extra costs on the industry since the Iran war began. (FT)

'Trump's Iran war pushes India to rekindle old friendship with Russia' Reuters article notes India and Russia are discussing resuming LNG trade amid Gulf supply disruptions, with New Delhi having approached Washington for potential sanctions waiver. (RTRS)

MACRO:

China launched a pair of investigations into US practices concerning supply chains and renewable products, in retaliation for US trade probes ahead of an expected presidential summit. The move, announced by the Ministry of Commerce on Friday, is a direct mirror of Washington’s Section 301 investigations. The investigation carries a six-month deadline, with a possible three-month extension, giving Beijing a legal justification for future countermeasures and leverage ahead of any talks. (BBG)

China is willing to strengthen economic and trade cooperation with the United States, its Commerce Minister Wang Wentao said during a meeting with USTR Greer, China's Commerce Ministry said in a statement. The discussion occurred during a World Trade Organization meeting in Cameroon on Thursday, the ministry said. (RTRS)

Senate Republicans and Democrats agreed early Friday to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security, moving to end a standoff over immigration enforcement in Congress that had led to missed paychecks for airport-security workers and long lines for travelers. (WSJ)

Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr said policymakers are well positioned to hold interest rates steady, as conflict in the Middle East and other factors complicate their ability to nudge inflation toward the 2% target. (BBG)

Equity funds suffered large outflows driven by US stocks, with bonds the only asset class with inflows this week, Bank of America says. US equities their biggest outflows in 13 weeks at $23.6b; European equities had largest outflow since April at $3.1b; Materials had largest outflow ever at $10.5b. US Treasuries 2-week inflow is largest since April at $19.7b. (BBG)

Japan's industry ministry has asked domestic wholesalers to switch to Brent crude oil pricing from the Dubai benchmark when setting gasoline prices, in an attempt to contain price increases, according to a document seen by Reuters on Friday. Vietnam, Indonesia and India have reached out to Japan for support, the document showed. (RTRS)

Japan’s trade minister said the country will sell oil from its reserves to domestic refiners as a general rule, signaling that the government isn’t currently planning to channel national supplies directly to other Asian nations seeking assistance. (BBG)

Japan industry ministry plans to propose increasing coal-fired power generation usage during the next fiscal year starting in April, as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran raises uncertainty over liquefied natural gas imports, it said in a document on Friday. (RTRS)

Japan’s aluminum buyers agreed to pay the highest premium in 11 years after the Iran war disrupted supplies, a cost that’s likely to stoke inflationary pressures at the factories which use the metal. (BBG)

Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Friday that the government was ready to take decisive steps on foreign exchange, warning against speculative trading linked to oil markets. (RTRS)

The BoJ released on Friday an updated estimate on Japan's natural rate of interest, which showed it in a range of around -0.9% to +0.5%. The range was largely unchanged from a previous estimate showing Japan's natural rate of interest in a range of -1.0% to +0.5%. (RTRS)

China's industrial firms reported stronger profit growth early in the year, reinforcing recovery signals in the world's second-largest economy even as the Middle East war threatens global growth. (RTRS)

China’s largest state-owned lenders reported flat earnings for 2025, as the industry grapples with government mandates to support a cooling economy and a protracted squeeze on profit margins. (BBG)

China's largest federation for science and technology professionals on Friday announced a boycott of a top AI conference after the California-based foundation that runs the gathering stopped accepting submissions from entities under U.S. sanctions. (RTRS)

China’s liquefied natural gas imports this month are on pace to hit the lowest level since 2018, according to ship-tracking data compiled by Kpler, as a price spike caused by the conflict in the Middle East curbs demand. (BBG)

Sulphur price surge creates windfall for China's struggling oil refiners. (RTRS)

Hong Kong is inviting a number of China-friendly central banks to participate in its gold-clearing system as part of a push to elevate the city as a major bullion-trading hub. (BBG)

ECB's Patsalides said the Bank should not rush to raise rates in response to surging energy costs, as its "baseline" outlook remains intact and there is no sign yet that inflation is becoming entrenched. (RTRS)

Spanish inflation jumped to its highest level since June 2024 due to the Iran war, supporting the case for the European Central Bank to raise interest rates. (BBG)

France reduced its public sector budget deficit more than expected last year as economic growth outpaced forecasts and tax hikes boosted government income, official data showed on Friday. (RTRS)

U.K. consumer sentiment worsened this month as the Iran war prompted renewed fears over price rises and added to concerns about the strength of the British economy. (WSJ)

Australia has a secure supply of fuel over the near term, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Friday, as he sought to reassure skittish households and businesses at a time of mounting reports of gasoline shortages. (BBG)

Consumer confidence in New Zealand fell in response to the uncertainty brought about by the Middle East conflict, ANZ-Roy Morgan data showed on Friday. (RTRS)

Procurement departments at South Korea semiconductor manufacturers including Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, and DB HiTek are conducting day-by-day checks on the availability and price movements of key materials, mobilizing all resources to prevent production disruptions. Helium is the most pressing concern. (The Elec via @jukan05)

Singapore set out plans on Friday to turn the city-state into a gold trading hub for the whole of Asia, with regulators and industry players working together to strengthen the market's trading, clearing and storage infrastructure. (RTRS)

India has slashed excise duties on petrol and diesel to protect consumers and rein in a potential spike in inflation, while imposing windfall taxes on aviation fuel and diesel exports, amid volatile global oil markets as a result of the Iran war. (RTRS)

Taiwan’s largest equity exchange-traded fund is on track for record inflows this month, as domestic investors defy the global turmoil sparked by the Iran war and pile into the technology-heavy product. (BBG)

Taiwan is facing its worst drought 75-years in the western half (most of population, entire semiconductor industry), media report. Premier Cho Jung-tai said the government’s goal is to avoid water rationing for industry and households before end-June. (LTN via @dnystedt)

Vietnamese airlines will significantly reduce flights and scale back operations from April as soaring jet fuel prices and supply constraints squeeze the industry, prompting carriers to focus on core routes as the Middle East conflict drags on. (BBG)

Thailand is tightening oversight of fuel pricing and supply as authorities ramp up efforts to address shortages across parts of the country. (BBG)

Thailand's manufacturing production index dropped 0.04% in February from a year earlier, the industry ministry said on Friday, weaker than analysts' forecasts. (RTRS)

GEOPOLITICAL:

Ukraine’s armed forces will participate in joint exercises with a coalition of Nato countries set up to defend Europe’s north-eastern flank, in a bid to deepen integration of Kyiv into Europe’s security architecture. (FT)

Russian President Putin has asked Russia’s oligarchs to donate to the country’s budget in a bid to stabilise the Kremlin’s strained finances, according to FT sources, as he pushes ahead with his costly and devastating invasion of Ukraine. (FT)

Ukraine risks running out of money to pay for its defense against Russia within two months as a multitude of factors converge to threaten tens of billions of euros in assistance from the country’s key donors. (BBG)

The U.S. Federal Maritime Commission (FMC) said on Thursday it is closely monitoring a surge in detentions of Panama-flagged vessels in China that appears tied to a Panama court ruling against Hong Kong-based CK Hutchison. (RTRS)

China has stationed obsolete supersonic fighters converted to attack drones at six air bases close to the Taiwan Strait, according to a new report by the Mitchell Institute for Aerospace Studies. (RTRS)

South Korean President Lee Jae Myung on Friday said the country would seek to reclaim wartime operational control from the U.S. at an early date, underscoring the need for greater military self-reliance. (RTRS)

EQUITIES:

SpaceX is preparing for a stock-market debut in mid-June, expected to raise $40 billion to $80 billion, potentially the largest IPO ever. Elon Musk plans an unconventional IPO, including investor visits to SpaceX facilities and allocating as much as a third or more of shares to individual investors. SpaceX is considering preferential treatment for investors in other Musk companies and unusual lockup times for early shareholders. (WSJ)

Customer testing of Huawei's new AI chip, designed to challenge Nvidia in the China market, has gone well and big tech giants including ByteDance and Alibaba plan to place orders, according to Reuters sources. Tech firms intend to use the new 950PR more extensively now, and are much happier that the chip is more compatible with Nvidia's CUDA software system and has better response speeds. Huawei plans to ship around 750,000 950PRs this year. (RTRS)

Four Chinese universities, including two linked to the People's Liberation Army, bought Super Micro Computer servers with restricted AI chips over the past year, procurement data shows, even as the U.S. clamps down on sales of some advanced processors to China. (RTRS)

A U.S. federal judge on Thursday halted the Trump administration’s designation of Anthropic as a supply-chain risk, issuing a ruling that the government trampled free-speech protections when it classified the AI company as a security threat and barred government use of its models. (WSJ)

Tether, the world’s largest stablecoin issuer, has hired KPMG to conduct an audit of its financial statements as it tries to build credibility ahead of a US expansion plan and potential fundraising. (FT)

A bipartisan group of senators introduced legislation Thursday that would require lawmakers and government employees to disclose any bets they place through prediction markets in a bid to prevent public officials from profiting on privileged information. (BBG)

Pernod Ricard and Brown-Forman are in talks to combine as alcohol companies contend with slowing sales. (WSJ)

TSMC’s 3nm and other advanced capacity remain so overwhelmed with orders that some chip design firms are mulling a move to Samsung Foundry, media report, adding designers say TSMC has prioritized AI-related orders from (GPU) Nvidia, AMD and (ASIC) Broadcom, Marvell first, then long-term customers 2nd, including Apple & MediaTek. (DigiTimes via @dnystedt)

Moonshot AI, one of China’s most promising artificial-intelligence startups, is considering changing its corporate structure to pave the way for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, according to WSJ sources. (WSJ)

Chinese bank stocks climbed in Hong Kong on Friday after Reuters reported that regulators are considering easing shareholder restrictions to broaden options for lenders seeking to raise capital. (RTRS)

Ping An of China Asset Management (Hong Kong) plans to ramp up purchases of short-term debt issued by Chinese banks to shield its investments from market volatility sparked by the Iran war. (BBG)

Santander said it has had a strong start to the year, with growth in customers and revenue, and backed its targets despite increasing global economic uncertainty. (WSJ)

Novartis said it agreed to buy Excellergy for up to $2 billion, bulking up its immunology portfolio with a biotech company that specializes in treatments for food allergy and other diseases. (WSJ)

Anyone that has read / followed the FT for a number of years will tell you the same thing. 

It has been crumbling for many years. 

What was once a politically agnostic institution has been destroyed by those pushing various leftist agendas. 

Such a shame.

quotedTweet.text:
By publishing this explicitly false story, the @FT has officially become tabloid trash for market participants.

Despite my direct, on-the-record denial of ever having advocated, explored, or espoused the idea that Chancellor-Bank of England statute serving as a prototype for a Treasury-Federal Reserve relationship, FT journalists manufactured a story with the headline, “Scott Bessent praised Bank of England as model for tighter oversight of the Federal Reserve.”

These pathetic journalists have clearly fabricated a story to give the impression that both I and the Trump Administration are setting “about restructuring the relationship… at a time when President Donald Trump has launched an unprecedented assault on the world’s most important central bank.”

Their mendacious assertion is based on vague statements from unnamed “financial industry executives familiar with the matter.”

In short, FT has literally manufactured an entirely fake policy position for me and the Administration. Other than furthering a maliciously false narrative of dysfunction and divisiveness, it baffles the mind as to why they would shred their already diminished journalistic credibility.

Over the past 10 years, I have written more than 20,000 words opining on the Federal Reserve decisions, personnel, structure, and modifications. Nowhere have I ever mentioned this ridiculous notion.

The Governor’s letters to the Chancellor have proven to be a useless and perfunctory device.

There is much to be said about the storied Bank of England, but any recreation of its operating framework on this side of the Atlantic has never been contemplated.

The shameful journalists and editors at the FT are shocking in their meretriciousness, lack of standards, and general intellectual libertinism. It is the worst tradition of Fleet Street to manufacture news rather than report on it.

They have brought irredeemable shame to their parent organization, Nikkei Inc., with whom I had previously held excellent relations.

In 2025, I laid out a comprehensive 6,000+ word review of each and every policy reform that I believe should be adopted by the Federal Reserve.

Read my actual, real thoughts on and proposals for Federal Reserve reform at the International Economy: https://t.co/0yQRXpMnK3

text:
« Il est temps de pointer du doigt ceux qui se disent ici "Patriotes" ou "souverainistes" mais qui roulent en vérité pour Trump, pour Poutine ou pour Xi Jinping. On les connaît ! La presse le révèle chaque jour : c’est l’AfD, le Rassemblement national, le Fidesz, Vox, Chega ou encore le PVV. Ils siègent à ESN, à l’ECR et chez les soi-disant Patriotes.

Mais quand on vote contre le soutien à l’Ukraine, quand on vote contre les sanctions pour la Russie, c’est ça être patriote ? Quand on soutient la politique MAGA de Trump, quand on soutient les géants du web américain contre le DSA, c’est ça être patriote ?

La vérité, c’est que ces partis ne défendent pas leur pays. Ils ne défendent pas leur peuple. Ils défendent des intérêts étrangers en affaiblissant l’Europe. Et je le rappelle au passage : de tels agissements dans nos États membres seraient passibles de condamnation pour haute trahison, d’intelligence avec l’ennemi ou d’atteintes aux intérêts fondamentaux de la Nation. »

David Sacks.. PayPal mafia.. billionaire VC.. handpicked by Trump to be the guy who shapes crypto and AI policy for the entire country..

130 days.. that's what he got.. the federal limit for a Special Government Employee.. and he used every single one..

in those 130 days..

Bitcoin crashed 40%.. no regulation passed.. the Clarity Act is still sitting in Congress.. AI companies got no framework.. crypto companies got no clarity.. and the guy who was supposed to fix all of it is now moving to a advisory board with no power..

the adults were in the room.. for 130 days.. and the room looks exactly the same as when they walked in..

Silicon Valley's most connected man got the most powerful tech policy role in the country.. and the biggest thing he shipped was a title.

🚨 Do you understand what the US just admitted..

850 Tomahawk missiles fired at Iran in 4 weeks..
Pentagon officials are "alarmed".. because the supply is running low..

here's what nobody's connecting..

these missiles took years to rebuild after burning through them in Iraq and Syria.. America just spent a massive chunk of its entire precision missile stockpile in one month..

at over $1M a missile..

and now they're pulling Patriot systems from South Korea and the Indo-Pacific to fill the gap..

think about what that means..

to fight Iran.. America just weakened its deterrence against China..
every missile that hit Tehran is one less missile protecting Taiwan..

I bet this is Iran's entire strategy.. bleed America dry before China makes its move.. drain the stockpile.. drain the treasury.. drain the political will..

the Houthis tested it with cheap drones.. Hezbollah tested it with rockets.. now Iran is doing it directly..

they've been running this playbook for years..
and it's working..

根據TrendForce最新調查，2025年起半導體晶圓代工及後段封裝測試成本持續上升，加上貴金屬原材料價格攀升，顯示驅動IC（DDIC）廠商面臨沉重成本壓力。部分業者已開始與面板客戶溝通，評估上調報價的可能性。

晶圓代工占DDIC成本六至七成，後段封測約占兩成。八吋產能長期未擴充，且受PMIC、Power Discrete等產品排擠，供給緊繃；十二吋成熟製程也因台系代工廠減少高壓產能，客戶轉向Nexchip，導致產能利用率高漲，價格呈上行趨勢。

此外，金凸塊材料因國際金價走高而成本增加，COF (Chip-on-Film) 與COG (Chip-on-Glass) 封測壓力尤為顯著。儘管部分廠商導入替代方案，短期仍難完全吸收。
若上游成本漲勢延續，DDIC漲價機率將提高，漲幅則取決於產品類型、應用市場（電視、監視器、筆電、手機）及客戶結構，最終可能傳導至面板廠與終端品牌。

路透社報導，華為新款AI晶片Ascend 950PR客戶測試順利，字節跳動、阿里巴巴等中國科技巨頭計劃下單採購。該晶片與英偉達CUDA軟體系統兼容性大幅提升，響應速度更快，開發者遷移模型更輕鬆。

儘管中國政府鼓勵國產晶片，先前旗艦昇騰910C未被大型科技公司廣泛採用。但這次950PR在推理工作負載表現優異，僅原始計算力小幅提升，卻更適合AI模型實際部署與OpenClaw等開源代理應用。

I’m very satisfied with the 1x and 5x cameras on the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra.
Both the image quality and rendering style are exactly where they should be.
The only weak point is the 3x.

My requirement is actually very simple.
For the S27 Ultra, I’d be completely satisfied if Samsung just upgrades the 3x camera with a sensor on the same level as the 5x.

Compared to previous generations, the biggest change in the S26 Ultra is that it presents image details in a much more “honest” way.
I would even say it’s one of the phones with the least noticeable AI intervention right now.

If you want to push this “honesty” to the extreme, there are a few ways to do it:
set softening to the maximum and shoot in 24MP mode; or use virtual aperture or reflector mode in Expert RAW. Both will give you a very clean and restrained image.

So what does “honest” actually mean?
It means the image is allowed to become naturally softer when you zoom in, instead of artificially painting in fake details or applying heavy sharpening that looks clean at first glance but falls apart on closer inspection.

Take a look at the original image and the zoomed-in details, and you’ll immediately understand how valuable and natural this kind of honesty really is.

@Teknium and @sudoingX 
Hermes at work!

quotedTweet.text:
Two Hermes agents wrote code together on Slack. reviewed each other's work. 

argued about architecture. one called the other's implementation "scattered." the other pushed back.

then i opened Telegram and asked: "what code did you and Daedalus work on?"

icarus remembered everything. the websocket broker. the missing methods. the critique. the rewrite. all from a completely different platform.

cross-platform persistent memory between two independent agents.

work happens on Slack. recall happens on Telegram. the memory carries. the relationship carries. the context carries. no vector database. no Redis. no infrastructure. just two agents that actually remember what they built together.

every agent framework in 2026 talks about memory. single agent memory across sessions. 

but two agents sharing persistent memory across platforms? that's the gap. arxiv published a paper about it two weeks ago calling it "the most pressing open challenge" in multi-agent systems.

it works now. only possible with Hermes 

https://t.co/xOhKDVVAfF

@Teknium  @NousResearch

Gemini 3.1 Flash Live 测试

响应速度确实是快
多语言自由切换

视频来自@livekit https://t.co/jNN3L4Szc0

quotedTweet.text:
Google 发布 Gemini 3.1 Flash Live 

全新语音模型 更快响应
更自然对话
记忆翻倍
支持 90 多种语言，200 多个国家和地区

Gemini 3.1 Flash Live是基于 Gemini 3 Pro 单独训练的独立模型，专门优化了延迟、语调感知和复杂任务执行

四大核心升级

① 响应更快，对话不再有"尴尬停顿"

以前跟 AI 语音对话，说完一句话要等一两秒它才回，节奏很割裂。

3.1 Flash Live 把延迟大幅压低了。在 Artificial Analysis 的测试中，最低思考级别下响应时间仅 0.96 秒。

② 能听懂你的语气，不再千篇一律

这是最值得关注的升级，3.1 Flash Live 比上一代 2.5 Flash Native Audio 更准确地识别音调、语速变化，能判断你是着急、困惑还是好奇，然后调整自己回复的长度和语气。

③ 对话记忆翻倍：长聊不失忆

上下文窗口 128K token，上下文跟踪长度是前代的 2 倍。

④ 嘈杂环境下的指令理解更好

在 Scale AI 的 Audio MultiChallenge 测试中得分 36.1%（开启思考模式），这个测试专门模拟真实语音中的打断、犹豫、背景噪音等干扰。

vivo X300 Ultraに低照度性能の意味でF値にがっかりしてるなら注意！

vivo X300 Ultraのペリスコはジンバル級ペリスコ。

CIPA7.0で７段手ブレ補正って意味だろうから
５段（X200 Ultra）からは

二段でssは４倍伸ばせるから、光量は４倍向上。

F値がF2.27→F2.67
28%光量低下するが
4*0.72≒2.9
だから、低照度なら2.9倍光量稼げるから。

低照度最強ペリスコの可能性ある。

🚨 Do you understand what UBS just did..

they locked $469 million in a real estate fund and told investors they can't have their money back for up to 3 years.. they're calling it a "withdrawal halt".. it has another name..

here's why this matters.. in June 2007, Bear Stearns froze two of their hedge funds.. told investors the same thing.. "temporary.. market conditions.. we'll sort it out.." 14 months later, Lehman Brothers collapsed and the entire global financial system went with it..

the "gate" on a fund is a confession.. it means UBS looked at their real estate portfolio, looked at how many people want out, and realised they don't have the liquidity to pay them..

commercial real estate has been quietly bleeding since COVID.. office vacancy at record highs.. WeWork gone.. buildings worth half what they were.. UBS just confirmed what the market has been pretending isn't true..

someone always knows before you do.. trust me..

and when they stop you from taking your own money, they've already made their decision.

quotedTweet.text: UBS has halted withdrawals from $469 million real estate fund for up to 3 years, per Reuters

quotedTweet.text:
🚨 let me tell you what's actually happening here..

Anthropic was founded in 2021 by people who LEFT OpenAI.. because OpenAI was moving too fast.. commercialising too aggressively.. not taking safety seriously enough..

their entire brand was built on one pitch: "we're the responsible ones.."

they raised over $7 billion from Amazon and Google on that pitch.. built Claude.. built a $61 billion valuation.. and kept saying the quiet part out loud "we might be building something dangerous, and we're doing it anyway.."

and now they want to sell shares to the public by October..

here's what that actually means.. the moment you let the public buy your stock, you answer to them.. quarterly earnings.. Wall Street analysts.. investors who don't care about safety, they care about growth.. pressure to ship faster, cut corners, beat OpenAI to market..

the "responsible AI company" will now be accountable to the same money that runs every other company they said they were different from..

they built their reputation on being the adults in the room..
turns out the adults just wanted a bigger paycheck.

the FBI spent two years investigating Hillary Clinton for using a personal email for government work.. it dominated every news cycle.. it decided an election.. "lock her up.."

What if you could save 25-50% of your AWS S3 bill just by running a few CLI commands? AND no longer have to worry about paying egress fees or api fees at all!

Let me explain:

* I built an unofficial Hugging Face CLI extension that analyzes your AWS S3 bucket and shows how much you'd save by migrating the data in the bucket to HF Storage Buckets. 
* If you like what you see, you can have it migrate the data for you too :)

What are Hugging Face Buckets? A mutable, S3-like object storage you can browse on the Hub, script from Python, or manage with the hf CLI. And because they are backed by Xet, they are especially efficient for ML artifacts that share content across files.

text:
Last year everyone spoke about over building of AI data centers, likely this year will start to demonstrate that there is not nearly enough compute to meet demand 

I think to degree to which AI is currently subsidized depends on the model, but agree with everything else here

quotedTweet.text:
Something that people making AI economics predictions should take into account is that 1) current AI use is heavily subsidized, 2) demand for smart, compute-heavy models is exploding w/ few price signals to discipline use, 3) we will be hitting serious compute constraints soon.

Translation: We have been living through a period of abundant AI; get ready for the age of compute scarcity. There will be more rationing, multi-tiered markets, new token allocation infrastructure, etc.

quotedTweet.text:
A new portrait of Chancellor Rachel Reeves has been unveiled.

It shows her preparing to deliver her first budget in October 2024 as the first female chancellor of the exchequer in the over 900 years the role has existed.

It is an oil painting by artist Sally Ward, and the Commons says she approached it "both a portrait and a history painting, showing an important moment in British political and parliamentary history".

Reeves says she is "deeply honoured" that it will be acquired by the Parliamentary Art Collection, and that she hopes it "serves as a reminder to every young woman and girl across the country that there should be no ceiling on their ambition".

It will be displayed in the Society of Women Artist’s summer exhibition in June, before its permanent hang in parliament's Portcullis House.

固态电池按真技术+量产进度+路线，当前最硬的几家如下（截至2026-03）：

一、宁德时代（300750）

• 路线：硫化物全固态（全球主流）

• 真技术：能量密度500Wh/kg+；研发团队超千人；10Ah验证平台、20Ah样品试制；界面阻抗、空气稳定性已工程化突破

• 进度：2027年小批量；车规级验证中

• 优势：全球产能+专利+产业链整合最强，最稳的量产龙头

二、比亚迪（002594）

• 路线：硫化物+氧化物双路线，主攻硫化物

• 真技术：自研电解质电导率10⁻³ S/cm；纳米钝化膜降界面阻抗90%；刀片固态融合，能量密度480Wh/kg、体积600Wh/L

• 进度：坪山中试线投产；重庆20GWh产线2026Q3小批量；2027示范装车

• 优势：整车+电池一体化，成本+装车落地最快

三、中创新航

• 路线：硫化物全固态

• 真技术：国内首条1GWh车规级全固态产线；良品率97%；能量密度380Wh/kg

• 进度：2026Q4千台级批量交付（国内最早）

• 优势：量产进度第一，绑定小鹏/广汽

四、亿纬锂能（300014）

• 路线：硫化物全固态

• 真技术：龙泉四号（60Ah动力）全固态下线；半/全固态全栈能力

• 进度：成都中试线；2026年样品验证、2027年小批量

• 优势：消费+动力双线，技术迭代快

五、赣锋锂业（002460）

• 路线：硫化物+聚合物+氧化物全覆盖

• 真技术：固态电解质自研+锂资源闭环；半固态已小批量；全固态能量密度400+Wh/kg

• 进度：中试+产线建设；2027年装车

• 优势：资源+材料+电池一体化，成本韧性极强

六、其他硬核玩家

• 丰田/松下：硫化物路线最早，专利深厚；2027-2028量产

• 清陶/卫蓝：氧化物路线，适配储能/商用车，稳定性强

• 辉能/鹏辉：半固态领先，2026年已装车

一句话总结

• 量产第一梯队：中创新航（2026Q4）、比亚迪（2026Q3）

• 技术+产能最强：宁德时代

• 资源+成本最优：赣锋锂业

• 路线最稳、落地最快：比亚迪

quotedTweet.text:
2022年碳酸锂冲至60万元/吨，天齐锂业、赣锋锂业合计净利超440亿；两年后价格暴跌至5.8万元/吨，两家合计亏损近百亿，锂电产业上演极致周期轮回。新能源车销量持续攀升，价格却反向大跌，这是资源争夺、技术迭代、产能博弈共同主导的产业规律，也是中国重资产制造业的典型缩影。

中国锂电产业链占据全球绝对主导，碳酸锂加工、正负极材料、电解液、隔膜产能均占全球七成以上，十五年深耕铸就完整产业体系。但产能过度集中且无序扩张，2021-2022年行业疯狂扩产，2023年起产能集中释放，全链陷入亏损，磷酸铁锂、电解液等环节价格跌幅超80%，产能利用率不足60%，陷入“增长式贫困”。

上游锂矿双雄走出差异化路径：天齐锂业蒋卫平两次“蛇吞象”收购海外顶级锂矿，手握低成本矿产，周期波动中盈亏弹性极大，后交棒女儿蒋安琪开启新阶段；赣锋锂业李良彬走“锂超市”路线，全球多点布局资源，周期低谷发力降本增效，押注固态电池与电池回收转型，亏损逐步收窄，其子亦进入核心管理层。

中游四大主材成行业“绞肉机”，比拼加工费与成本控制。磷酸铁锂行业连续亏损超36个月，湖南裕能靠规模与大客户绑定稳坐头名，德方纳米凭技术立足却深陷亏损，万润新能押注高压实产品寻求突破；电解液率先迎来供需反转，六氟磷酸锂价格大幅反弹；隔膜行业恩捷股份以价战挤压对手，谋求寡头垄断；负极材料聚焦硅碳升级，头部企业抢占技术先机。

2025年碳酸锂走出V型反转，政策减产、储能需求爆发、出口抢单三重驱动，价格回升至15万元/吨左右，行业出清信号显现。同时固态电池成下一代竞争核心，中日韩技术角逐激烈，中国面临专利与产能重塑挑战；锂资源地缘政治风险加剧，产业加速推进回收与资源多元化。

Wedbush analyst @DivesTech in new $TSLA note:

"We continue to believe that SpaceX and Tesla will eventually merge into one company in 2027 with the groundwork already in place for both operations to become one organization. Tesla already owns a stake in SpaceX after the company’s $2 billion investment in xAI got converted to SpaceX shares following SpaceX’s acquisition of xAI earlier this year initially tying both of Musk’s ventures closer together but still represents <1% of SpaceX’s expected valuation.

The recent announcement of a joint Terafab facility between SpaceX and Tesla further ties both operations together making it more feasible to merge operations given the now existing overlap being built out across the two with this the first step. While there are plenty of regulatory hurdles from the FTC and DOJ, this remains Musk’s end goal by owning 25% of TSLA over time as it enters the most important growth chapter in the company’s history. 

Musk wants to own and control more of the AI ecosystem and step by step the holy grail could be combining SpaceX and Tesla in some way to give the connected tissue between both disruptive tech stalwarts looking to lead the AI Revolution."

在美国，这样做量化几乎是不可以的，那是做市商的家底：

为什么说在厉害的散户在量化面前都不值一提，就拿梁文峰（幻方量化）来说，量化收割散户核心是技术碾压+规则套利+情绪操控+止损猎杀，用毫秒级速度、资金优势，精准捕捉散户行为弱点完成闭环。
一、底层技术壁垒（散户无法抗衡）
- 极速交易：服务器托管交易所机房，延迟0.3毫秒（散户约200毫秒），可插队抢单 。
- 订单簿嗅探：实时读取Level-2盘口，预判散户挂单，抢先成交赚微差 。
- AI+超算：自研“萤火”超算+大模型，秒级处理舆情、资金、盘口数据，比散户快600倍。
二、集合竞价：开盘即收割（典型流程）
1. 9:15 挂虚假大单：挂数倍于真实需求的买单/卖单，制造强势假象。
2. 9:24:50 闪电撤单：0.5秒内撤掉所有假单，开盘价虚高/虚低。
3. 散户接盘：散户按虚高价格市价抢筹，开盘即被套。
三、日内收割完整流程（以小盘股为例）
1. 提前锁定目标：AI筛选散户扎堆、流通盘小、止损密集的标的。
2. 诱多拉升：小单慢推股价，营造突破，散户加仓；同时融券做空。
3. 峰值砸盘：散户追涨最疯狂时，瞬间砸空单，股价快速跳水。
4. 止损猎杀：精准砸到散户集中止损位（如整数关下方0.5%），触发批量割肉 。
5. 低位接回：在恐慌低点买回平仓，完成“高卖低买”闭环。
四、核心收割手法（精准打击散户）
- 高频插队：散户挂单瞬间，以0.001元差价抢先成交，再微利卖出 。
- 情绪反向收割：NLP监控股吧，“加仓/涨停”暴增时立即抛售 。
- 止损点猎杀：大数据定位散户止损密集区，集中砸盘触发踩踏 。
- 封板诱多：涨停板挂巨单再撤单，制造封单松动，诱散户接盘后瞬间砸开。
- 融券闭环：拉升时融券，砸盘时平仓，日内无风险套利。
五、为什么散户必亏
- 速度差：你还在看盘，它已完成百笔交易。
- 信息差：它掌握订单簿、舆情、资金流向，你只有K线。
- 行为被建模：你的挂单、止损、追涨习惯，被AI精准预测。
- 资金碾压：百亿级资金可短期操控小盘股走势。
梁文峰的幻方策略本质是逆向交易：在散户贪婪时卖出、恐慌时买入，赚取情绪与错误定价的钱，散户在这样的条件下只有被收割！

Cline 推出 Cline Kanban：把 CLI 环境中的 Coding Agents 工作迁移至可视化看板，支持 Claude Code、Codex 及 Cline 等多种 Agents，实现 CLI 无关、模型无关的统一管理

核心功能
· 可视化任务管理：以 Kanban 卡片形式呈现任务，支持创建、编辑、链接和启动。侧边栏内置 Agents 可直接响应自然语言指令，帮助用户快速拆分大型项目、自动建立依赖链，并实现最大化并行执行。
· Worktree 隔离：每项任务在独立 Git 工作树中运行，避免主分支污染；点击卡片即可实时查看代码 diff，并支持 inline 评论。
· 依赖链与自主执行：卡片间可手动或自动链接成链路，支持 auto-commit 模式，让 Agents 自主完成复杂依赖顺序与并行任务。
· Git 原生集成：导航栏一键打开完整 Git 界面，支持浏览提交历史、分支切换、fetch/pull/push 等操作，无需离开应用。
· 外部工具兼容：无缝对接 Linear MCP 与 gh CLI 等，进一步简化工作流。

Polymarket 3 月 30 日起扩大 Taker Fee 覆盖范围。

之前只有 Crypto 和 Sports 收费，现在新增 8 个品类：

Weather 1.25%
Mentions 1.56%
Economics 1.50%
Culture 1.25%
Finance 1.00%（Maker Rebate 最高 50%）
Politics 1.00%
Tech 1.00%
Crypto 1.56% → 1.80%（上调）

地缘政治永久免费。费率是动态的——概率越接近 50% 收得越多，越接近 0 或 1 越便宜。

跑量化策略的人建议自检一下：

1. 你的策略是 Taker 还是 Maker？Maker 依然零费用 + rebate
2. 你的入场价分布在哪个区间？高概率区（>0.80）实际费率远低于峰值
3. 单笔利润能不能覆盖新增成本？

查了一遍自己跑的几个策略品类，高概率区入场的策略实际费率远低于峰值，整体影响在个位数百分比。

结论：Taker  策略影响有限（尤其高概率区入场的），但做市策略的结构性优势更大了——Taker 成本上升 = spread 扩大 = Maker 利润空间增加。

费率表见图。官方链接见评论区👇

卧槽，钉钉宣布其 CLI 直接开源

一行命令调用钉钉全部产品能力

首批开放 AI 表格、日历、日志、待办、机器人、通讯录、DING 消息、考勤、开放平台文档、工作台共 10 项核心产品能力...

原生支持 Claude Code、小龙虾 等主流 AI Agent 执行环境。

三个关键设计：

① 所有输出都是结构化 JSON：AI Agent 不需要解析自然语言返回值，直接拿到结构化数据就能用。

② 内置 Agent Skills： 安装脚本会自动把每个产品的 SKILL.md 文件安装到 ~/.agents/skills/dws 目录下。Claude Code、小龙虾等会自动发现这个目录，开箱即用。

③ 支持 dry-run：加 --dry-run 可以预览操作但不执行，Agent 在执行敏感操作前可以先确认。

应该是国内首个面向 AI Agent 开源全产品能力的国民级应用...🫡

首批开放 10 项能力

"即将推出"的能力：文档、邮箱、AI 听记、钉盘、视频会议、Teambition、AI 应用、直播、技能市场。 https://t.co/MwWwC1mfuT

ByteRover 为 OpenClaw 推出的原生记忆插件，从 Skill 层下沉到 Context Engine 层，实现真正的有状态长期记忆。

三层记忆架构
· Context Tree：深度结构化知识库
· Workspace Memory：核心规则、偏好、指南
· Daily Memory：会话笔记

自动化循环
1. 实时注入：每次模型运行前自动将相关知识注入系统 prompt
2. 即时保存：每次运行后将新决策写回记忆树
3. 内存兜底：监控上下文窗口，在强制压缩前自动提取保存关键信息
4. 每日复盘：早9点 cron 任务自动提取昨日架构决策和模式

性能指标
· LoCoMo / LongMemEval 检索准确率：92.2%
· 轻量模型下仍保持 90% 准确率
· 知识更新准确率：99%
· 跨会话召回率：84%

关键特性
· Git-like 状态化：语义层级、人类可读、可 diff、Git 友好的 Markdown 文件
· 本地优先：默认本地存储，无需账号；支持团队共享、版本控制、回滚
· 历史兼容：一键将现有 memory/ 目录的扁平日志转换为结构化 Context Tree

Jefferies on recent Bloomberg report on Nintendo below. 

I agree with Jefferies. Bloomberg reminds me of an old Pacific Crest analyst who consistently published estimates well above consensus on EA while maintaining a sell rating on the stock, allowing him to cut numbers later. Intellectually dishonest.

"Ignore Bloomberg Noise; Pokopia, Galaxy Movie, Mario Game are Reasons to BUY"

"Pokopia's 2.2m 4-day launch (~12.6% attach) and The Mario Movie (Apr 1) signal the content cycle accelerates sharply from here. Maintain BUY." 

"Bloomberg report citing a >30% NS2 production cut comes from a correspondent whose prior Nintendo and Sony claims have been formally denied by both companies on multiple occasions. Hardware is stron—17.4m units in 7 months vs. Switch 1's 17.8m over 13."

"Bloomberg (March 20) reported Nintendo is cutting NS2 output by over 30%, citing weak US holiday demand. Nintendo has declined to comment on the report. The article's author in October 2025 reported that Nintendo was asking suppliers to prepare for up to 25m NS2 units in FY26. Back then, we flagged that this figure exceeded company guidance of 15m and our forecast of 18.5m, and cautioned against placing any weight on the report. We reiterate that caution now."

"This reporter has a documented history of inaccurate reporting on Nintendo and Sony, prompting formal, on-record denials from the companies—an unusual step for Japanese corporates. Nintendo issued a denial in Sep'21 following a Bloomberg report (by Mochizuki) claiming the company was distributing 4K Switch development kits to 3P developers. Nintendo subsequently denied a report on NS2 wholesale pricing in Japan. Sony similarly refuted a Mochizuki report forecasting a PS5 production cut—a prediction that materially preceded a period of strong PS5 volume delivery. The pattern of manufacturer-level rebuttal is relevant to how investors should regard the current report."

🚨 Do you understand what UBS just did..

they locked $469 million in a real estate fund and told investors they can't have their money back for up to 3 years.. they're calling it a "withdrawal halt".. it has another name..

here's why this matters.. in June 2007, Bear Stearns froze two of their hedge funds.. told investors the same thing.. "temporary.. market conditions.. we'll sort it out.." 14 months later, Lehman Brothers collapsed and the entire global financial system went with it..

the "gate" on a fund is a confession.. it means UBS looked at their real estate portfolio, looked at how many people want out, and realised they don't have the liquidity to pay them..

commercial real estate has been quietly bleeding since COVID.. office vacancy at record highs.. WeWork gone.. buildings worth half what they were.. UBS just confirmed what the market has been pretending isn't true..

someone always knows before you do.. trust me..

and when they stop you from taking your own money, they've already made their decision.

Some traditional banking services weren't built for the internet economy.
They were built for borders.

But your business doesn't have borders.
• Your customers pay in crypto and cards.
• Your suppliers want fiat and stablecoins.
• Traditional banks can take days to move $500.
And you shouldn't have to be your own accountant either.

We built INFINI to change this.
An AI-Powered Financial Platform for Global Business — where an AI agent manages your finances, so you can focus on what actually grows your business.

with Infini you can:

1. Pay your team and vendors globally

Your team gets corporate cards funded by stablecoins.
Pay vendors globally in fiat (USD, EUR, etc.) via wire/local payment networks.
Spend anywhere Visa is accepted — SaaS subscriptions, cloud infra, ad platforms.
Our AI auto-categorizes every transaction into a clean, categorized ledger.
Spend less time deciphering cryptic bank statements.

2. Get paid like a local — anywhere in the world

Collect with Global Virtual Accounts (USD, EUR, GBP).
One checkout API for Cards, Stablecoins, and local payment methods — all in.
Crypto acquiring at 0.3% — not the 3% you're used to.
Faster settlement. Fewer drop-offs. More revenue.

3. Manage your treasury with one dashboard
Allocate between spending, saving, and earning in seconds.
Put idle balances to work with industry-leading yield — your cash shouldn't sit at 0%.
AI aims to turn hours of reconciliation into minutes.

You didn't start a company to do bookkeeping — so let the AI run your back office.
Your financial stack shouldn't be 6 tabs and a prayer.

It should be one OS.

Introducing AA-AgentPerf - the hardware benchmark for the agent era.

Key details:
➤ Real agent workloads, not synthetic queries: we’ve captured real coding agent trajectories where our agents used up to 200 turns and worked with sequence lengths >100K tokens
➤ Production optimizations allowed: KV cache reuse, disaggregated prefill/decode, speculative decoding - we’re allowing the optimizations that labs and inference providers are serving in production so that we can capture what real deployments should look like
➤ Measures what developers need to know: Max concurrent users at each target output speed, expressed per accelerator, per kW TDP, per $/hr, and per rack
➤ Built for every kind of scale: designed to measure systems from a single accelerator up to a full rack, and to fairly evaluate every architecture from DRAM-only designs to SRAM-only designs and everything in between
➤ Live now: we’re announcing AA-AgentPerf today and opening submissions of configurations for benchmarking effective immediately. The models supported at launch are gpt-oss-120b and DeepSeek V3.2. We’ll be publishing results on a rolling basis.

AA-AgentPerf is a benchmark for real-world performance of AI accelerator hardware. We’re benchmarking inference of particular models on a specific system with a specific config (ie. inference stack, parallelism config and more).

AA-AgentPerf has been shaped by our work with inference providers and engagement with AI accelerator companies, developers, and enterprise buyers over the past year. Our goal is for anyone deploying models - whether buying or leasing accelerators - to be able to use AA-AgentPerf as the definitive resource for understanding real-world hardware performance.

AA-AgentPerf results will primarily be expressed as a maximum number of concurrent users serviceable at a given per-user token output speed (and vice versa). We will combine these results with several dimensions that are important to developers and customers: 
➤ Users per accelerator: the most basic view - how many users can be serviced by a single accelerator at each output speed.
➤ Users per kW TDP: provides context on how power-efficient the accelerators are. 
➤ Users per unit rental cost: provides context on how cost-efficient each accelerator is. 
➤ Users per rack: provides context on how space-efficient the accelerators are.

With these metrics, organizations will be able to make a more informed decision, maximizing performance given any constraints on power, cost, or space. For example, a certain accelerator may be more attractive if it can feed 50 tokens/s output to more users than another accelerator, while using the same power & space, and costing the same per hour of use.

We expect initial results to be available within the next 1-2 weeks, after submissions from hardware providers and QA from our team. Results will be visible at https://t.co/WPFdWzokO6

Get to know the evaluation methodology more closely at https://t.co/GamigkpfA4 https://t.co/mMcFCexJru

Okay, @gdb is team CLI all the way. @garrytan thinks MCPs suck.

So we hit the streets of SF to see if the city agreed.

We posed a simple question: MCP or CLI?

- Basically everyone under the age of 35 said CLI
- One person said MCP was as bloated as Java 
- & unsurprisingly, numerous people told us to touch grass

Final score- MCP: 3 vs CLI: 17

SF has spoken, and @composio listened.

Our universal CLI is now live!

title_zh: 机器人灵巧手，全掌触觉一直是个难题，视觉遮挡时需要靠触觉做掌内操作，但很难找到好的全掌覆盖方案。团队换了个思路——用触觉手套，人手和仿生手都能穿戴，同样的操作能
summary_zh: 机器人灵巧手，全掌触觉一直是个难题，视觉遮挡时需要靠触觉做掌内操作，但很难找到好的全掌覆盖方案。团队换了个思路——用触觉手套，人手和仿生手都能穿戴，同样的操作能采到近似的触觉数据，采集效率比遥操作高了很多，并且磨损了换手套就行。仿生手做到了跟人手1:1尺寸，自由度提升64%同时体积比 ​​​ ...展开

summary_zh: 独游攀岩模拟器《#孤山独影# 》销量破50万近日，独立游戏开发商The Game Bakers宣布，旗下攀岩模拟游戏《孤山独影》销量已突破50万份。据了解，今年2月初时，官方曾公布游戏在发售首个周末售出20万份。这意味着在过去数周内，本作销量已翻倍有余。目前，《孤山独影》登陆了PC和PlayStation 5平台。 ​​​

text:
Chinese OpenSource models are gonna mug Anthropic & OpenAI like they never existed before

The coding gap between open and closed-source is practically gone

GLM-5.1 gives the almost the same comparable coding performance that goes toe-to-toe with Claude Opus, but a roughly 10x lower cost of Opus

What makes it worse for Anthropic is GLM and other OpenSource models are shipping much faster, and the leap in quality with every update is huge

Turns out, GLM models hallucinate significantly less and have better tool-calling performance than Claude Opus and GPT-4.5

They are both building in the exact same lane and while Anthropic retains a very slight edge today, GLM’s inference efficiency is doing things Anthropic just can’t really touch

There is a massive silent majority of devs already running GLM models locally to fully replace Claude, or just paying for much cheaper API inference... they just aren't posting about it on 𝕏 yet

The shift is already happening

昨晚睡前给 AI 布置了一个任务，让它帮我做一个社交媒体管理工具，能够一键发布到多平台，也能从多平台中将互动信息收集回来。提示词大概写了一两千字，样式让它直接一比一复刻 Twitter，一觉醒来，完成度非常高，基本能用了😄

自动化登录流程太麻烦了，我让它通过 Chrome devtools MCP 直接读取了我在浏览器上的已有登录态，然后在数据抓取时将 cookies/localstorage 再传递给 playwright，走 headless 完成自动化。

网页元素分析的工作难度也比较高，我让它直接参考开源的 opencli —— gihtub 接近 1w stars，它做到了几乎所有主流媒体网站的信息抓取能力。其实原理也很简单， opencli 先让用户先安装它提供的 Chrome 插件，插件具备 page debugging 能力，原理跟 Chrome devtools MCP 一样，都走的 cdp 协议驱动，它支持对网页的 DOM/Request 做劫持和分析。

样式复刻就更绝了🤣。我让 AI 一边写代码，一边打开 Chrome 去对比当前网页和 Twitter 网页的 DOM 和 style 差异，并且做完之后还得 review 了三次，结果就是，还原效果异常地高！

这个程序，我在五六年前也写过，当时前前后后调了十来天，适配了四个社交媒体，全部是体力活儿，后面也因为媒体网站改版弃坑了。今天有了 AI 之后，睡一觉的功夫，效果就出来了，生产力简直是爆炸啊！💥

[实战指南] AI Agent 评估准备清单 -- 来自 @LangChain 
https://t.co/sG2dNrxppW

- 评估前的必要准备

1. 人工审查优先：在构建任何评估基础设施前，先手动审查 20-50 个真实 Agent Traces。这比任何自动化系统更能揭示失败模式。
2. 明确成功标准：如果两位专家无法就"通过/失败"达成一致，任务定义需要细化。
  · 模糊示例："很好地总结这份文档"
  · 清晰示例："从会议记录中提取 3 个主要行动项，每项不超过20字，包含提及的负责人"
3. 能力评估与回归评估分离：
  · 能力评估：回答"它能做什么？"，初始通过率较低，用于推动进步
  · 回归评估：回答"它还能用吗？"，应保持接近 100% 通过率，防止功能倒退
4. 错误归因分析：应投入 60-80% 的评估精力在错误分析上。建立失败分类体系（提示问题、工具设计问题、模型局限、工具失败、数据缺口等），针对性修复。
5. 单一领域专家负责制：避免委员会式设计，由一位领域专家担任质量仲裁者。
6. 排除基础设施干扰：Witan Labs 团队发现，单个提取 bug 使基准测试从 50% 提升至 73%。在归咎于智能体前，先排除超时、API 响应格式错误、缓存失效等问题。

- 评估层级选择

三个评估层级：
1. 单步（Run）：单个工具调用或决策 -- 架构稳定后，用于调试特定步骤
2. 完整回合（Trace）：从输入到最终输出的完整执行 -- 大多数团队应从此开始
3. 多回合（Thread）：跨多个交互的上下文保持 -- 对话式智能体，需使用 N-1 测试策略

Trace 级评估的三个维度：
· 最终响应：输出是否正确有用？
· 执行轨迹：是否采取了合理路径？（不必完全匹配预期，只需有效）
· 状态变更：是否创建了正确的产物？（文件、数据库更新、会议安排等）
关键提醒：对于"行动型"智能体，务必验证实际状态变更，而非仅检查输出文本说"已完成"。

- 数据集构建

1. 任务无歧义性：每个任务必须有参考解证明其可解。避免"帮我找去纽约的好航班"这类模糊请求。
2. 正负案例平衡：既要测试"应该搜索时是否搜索"，也要测试"不该搜索时是否避免搜索"，防止过度优化。
3. 按智能体类型定制：
  · 编码智能体：包含确定性测试套件（单元测试）+ 质量评分标准
  · 对话智能体：多维度标准（任务完成度 + 交互质量：同理心、清晰度）
  · 研究智能体：包含依据性检查（声明是否有来源支持）+ 覆盖度检查
4. 冷启动策略：如缺乏生产数据，手动创建 20-50 个覆盖关键维度的示例，运行并修正后作为可靠基准。质量优于数量。
5. 持续改进飞轮：
  · 日常 Dogfooding：团队主动在实际工作流中压力测试
  · 改编外部基准：从 Terminal Bench 或 BFCL 等选取相关任务适配
  · 手写行为测试：针对特定行为编写聚焦测试

- 评分器设计

评分原则：
· 使用结构化评分标准（Rubric）
· 明确定义评分维度与惩罚项
· 支持布尔值、数值或分类输出格式

- 实践建议总结

· 起步策略：从简单的端到端评估开始，即使架构仍在变化，也能立即获得基线信号。
· 资源分配：60-80% 精力投入错误分析，20-40% 用于构建评估基础设施。
· 避免过早优化：仅在简单评估方法无法捕获真实失败时，才增加复杂度。
· 状态验证：对于执行操作的智能体，始终验证实际状态变更，而非仅检查输出文本。
· N-1 测试：对于多回合评估，使用真实对话的前 N-1 回合作为前缀，让智能体仅生成最后一回合，避免完全合成模拟的误差累积问题。

Deep Agents 评估体系构建指南

来自 @LangChain 团队 @Vtrivedy10 的深度分享：
最佳 Agent 评估直接衡量生产中真正关心的具体行为，而非盲目堆积数量。评估不仅是测试，更是塑造 Agent 行为的“向量”——每项保留的评估都会长期施加压力，引导系统提示、工具描述和整体设计。

评估构建原则
· 从生产行为逆向设计：先明确目标行为（如跨多文件检索、5+工具链式调用），再精准挑选或编写可验证的评估。
· 自文档化+标签化：每项评估添加 docstring 说明测量能力，并打标签（如 tool_use），便于分组运行和维护。
· 迭代闭环：通过 LangSmith 痕迹分析失败模式，团队共享责任，定期审视并精简评估，避免“评估越多越好”的幻觉。

数据来源
· dogfooding：日常使用中每处错误都转化为新评估，并优化上下文工程。
· 外部基准适配：精选并修改 BFCL、Terminal Bench 2.0 等任务，置于沙箱环境运行。
· 手工编写：针对孤立行为（如 read_file 工具）的焦点测试。

评估分类（taxonomy）
按实际测试内容而非来源分组，获得“中间视角”而非单一总分或单个用例。团队定义的类别包括：
· file_operations：文件工具（读写编辑 ls grep glob）、并行调用、分页
· retrieval：多文件信息查找、搜索策略、多跳文档合成
· tool_use：工具选择、多步链式调用、跨轮状态跟踪
· memory：回忆种子上下文、提取隐式偏好、持久化信息
· conversation：模糊请求的澄清提问、多轮对话正确动作
· summarization：上下文溢出处理、摘要触发、压缩后信息恢复
· unit_tests：SDK底层机制（提示透传、中断配置等）

指标体系
· 第一优先级：正确性  
  内部评估用自定义断言（如“是否并行工具调用”）；外部基准用精确匹配；语义任务用LLM-as-judge。
· 第二优先级：效率（正确性达标后对比）  
  关键指标：延迟比（latency ratio）、解决率（solve rate，归一化预期步骤）、工具调用数、步骤数。  
· 参考基准：理想轨迹（ideal trajectory）——最少必要动作、无冗余、并行优化的路径。 
  示例：同一正确任务，“低效轨迹”可能增加2步、1次多余调用，导致更高延迟、成本和潜在失败风险。

执行框架
· pytest + GitHub Actions CI，在干净环境中运行。
· 按标签运行子集（节省成本），聚焦特定场景（如文件操作密集型）。
· 完整开源于 Deep Agents 仓库，支持多模型并行实验。

Microsoft is putting together a new team to focus on building 100% native apps for Windows 11.

And honestly, it’s long overdue.

The real problem with Windows 11 isn’t ads, but the shift from native apps to web apps.

The rushed adoption of web over native frameworks has hurt Windows 11. From WhatsApp to Weather to the pre-installed video editor, everything is a web app running inside Edge or a Chromium container.

Open Task Manager and search for WebView -- you’re going to find many apps are web-based, including Outlook.

And this doesn’t even include the Electron apps in Windows 11.

summary_zh:
#NeurIPS含金量归零# NeurIPS这下摊上大事了！#科技先锋官# 

以「合规」为借口的歧视性政策，引发了中国乃至全球学术圈的强烈不满与抗议。

title: 机器人灵巧手，全掌触觉一直是个难题，视觉遮挡时需要靠触觉做掌内操作，但很难找到好的全掌覆盖方案。团队换了个思路——用触觉手套，人手和仿生手都能穿戴，同样的操作能采到近似的触觉数据，采集效率比遥操作高了
title_zh: 机器人灵巧手，全掌触觉一直是个难题，视觉遮挡时需要靠触觉做掌内操作，但很难找到好的全掌覆盖方案。团队换了个思路——用触觉手套，人手和仿生手都能穿戴，同样的操作能采到近似的触觉数据，采集效率比遥操作高了
summary_zh:
机器人灵巧手，全掌触觉一直是个难题，视觉遮挡时需要靠触觉做掌内操作，但很难找到好的全掌覆盖方案。团队换了个思路——用触觉手套，人手和仿生手都能穿戴，同样的操作能采到近似的触觉数据，采集效率比遥操作高了很多，并且磨损了换手套就行。
仿生手做到了跟人手1:1尺寸，自由度提升64%同时体积比 ​​​

一颗真正能打的 10X，一直是所有厂商都想装进手机里的镜头。因为真正的手机长焦竞争，终局一定是 10X。它不只是长焦能力的上限，更是手机完整影像能力的最后一块拼图。

但这么多年，行业始终没能把一颗真
title_zh:
一颗真正能打的 10X，一直是所有厂商都想装进手机里的镜头。因为真正的手机长焦竞争，终局一定是 10X。它不只是长焦能力的上限，更是手机完整影像能力的最后一块拼图。

但这么多年，行业始终没能把一颗真
summary_zh:
一颗真正能打的 10X，一直是所有厂商都想装进手机里的镜头。因为真正的手机长焦竞争，终局一定是 10X。它不只是长焦能力的上限，更是手机完整影像能力的最后一块拼图。

但这么多年，行业始终没能把一颗真正能打的高画质 10X 做进手机。做不进去，就只能挂在外面。所以，增倍镜成了过去最无奈、也最诚 ​​​

#一加15T# 已经发布了，这次的升级相较于 13T，除了硬件常规升级，影像、屏幕和续航都有进步。

外观和手感上，一加 15T 更倾向小屏党。除了 6.32英寸的小机身之外，R角相比之前做的更大，单手握持状态下不会硌到手掌，50:50的天平配重下，也不会有头重脚轻的感觉。喜欢边吃饭边单手刷手机的用户拿着更 ​​​

title:

title:
换大冰箱之后感觉真好啊，每次开冰箱门都觉得特别幸福😊

当时换这个冰箱的核心原因，就是之前的老冰箱经常出现冰箱门没关严，隔了一天发现里边的食物融化又结冰的情况，非常头疼。所以换新冰箱的时候特意选了一款

summary_zh:
Token 似乎有了正式的中文译名“词元”，之前“模元”、“头啃”各种叫法满天飞，同个词能翻出好几个版本。
说实话“词元”这个译法我觉得合理，字面刚好踩中了它 “承载语义的最小基础单元” 的本质，而且 NLP 圈很早就通用这个说法，不算是硬造的新词。但服务器的 Token 是不是也得赶快出个标准中文 ​​​

summary_zh:
【#科普氪#｜重复克隆也有极限】
日本科学家开展的一项为期20年的小鼠研究表明，哺乳动物无法无限进行重复克隆。有性生殖对于消除哺乳动物克隆体中可能积累的大规模基因突变是必要的。相关研究3月25日发表于《自然-通讯》。

title:

summary_zh:
开源项目推荐：Type4Me，作者号称是 Typeless 平替

macOS 语音输入工具，本地/云端双引擎识别，大模型文本优化，数据全部存在本地。

title: 我现在所有 skills 的脚本，默认用 bun 执行，因为 bun 可以直接执行 TypeScript，不需要预先编译，这带来的好处就是如果出错了，Agent 可以快速定位到代码里面的问题，然后直接
title_zh: 我现在所有 skills 的脚本，默认用 bun 执行，因为 bun 可以直接执行 TypeScript，不需要预先编译，这带来的好处就是如果出错了，Agent 可以快速定位到代码里面的问题，然后直接
summary_zh:
我现在所有 skills 的脚本，默认用 bun 执行，因为 bun 可以直接执行 TypeScript，不需要预先编译，这带来的好处就是如果出错了，Agent 可以快速定位到代码里面的问题，然后直接修改代码再次运行，如果是打包后的代码这就很难打补丁。

任何语言或者环境，Agent 友好才是王道！ ​​​​类似的还有 pyt ​​​

title:
推荐李继刚老师的系列技能：

ljg-card 内容铸卡
将内容转为 PNG 视觉卡片（长图、信息图、海报）。信息图模式会根据内容密度、结构和情绪自动生成独特的视觉构图，没有固定模板，形式服务于内容。
title_zh:
推荐李继刚老师的系列技能：

ljg-card 内容铸卡
将内容转为 PNG 视觉卡片（长图、信息图、海报）。信息图模式会根据内容密度、结构和情绪自动生成独特的视觉构图，没有固定模板，形式服务于内容。
summary_zh:
推荐李继刚老师的系列技能：

ljg-card 内容铸卡
将内容转为 PNG 视觉卡片（长图、信息图、海报）。信息图模式会根据内容密度、结构和情绪自动生成独特的视觉构图，没有固定模板，形式服务于内容。

ljg-learn 概念解剖
从八个方向切开一个概念（历史、辩证、现象、语言、形式、存在、美感、元反思）， ​​​

title:
问：在什么情况下应该用 slash command？什么情况下应该用skill呢？

答：slash command 是用户主动触发的，比如 /init 之类
Skills 是由 Agent 自主调用
title_zh:
问：在什么情况下应该用 slash command？什么情况下应该用skill呢？

答：slash command 是用户主动触发的，比如 /init 之类
Skills 是由 Agent 自主调用
summary_zh:
问：在什么情况下应该用 slash command？什么情况下应该用skill呢？

答：slash command 是用户主动触发的，比如 /init 之类
Skills 是由 Agent 自主调用的，它会检查 Skills 列表，看当前任务是不是要用 Skill，以及该用哪个 Skill。

当然 Skill 也可以当 slash command 用 ​​​

summary_zh:
Anthropic 又 tm 改账号的额度消耗方式了

与此同时，CodeX 全天候接下来一周所有用户都有两倍的额度，Open AI 🐂🍺

总额度是不变的，但是在高峰期，你的账号额度会消耗得更快。

所以逼用户把任务放在非高峰期去跑，而且这里的高峰期是按 PT 和 GMT 定义的。

所以美国和咱们这边的高峰期是一样 ​​​

summary_zh:
果子突然变得这么开放

有消息称在 iOS 27 里，Siri 将变成一个 AI 中枢，可以把请求转发给通过 App Store 安装的第三方大模型或 AI 助手

Siri 本身会迎来一次大改版，新增独立应用（Siri app）、更接近聊天机器人的界面，以及系统级的“Ask Siri”入口（例如在系统菜单中一键把当前内容发给 Siri）。 ​​​

title_zh: 回复@LiquidSword:显卡算子不支持就必须反量化. 甚至CPU也是一样的, 如果CPU指令集不支持低精度数据进行运算, 那就要么反量化, 要么用纯软方法来模拟硬件运算(巨慢).   而且反量化
summary_zh: 回复@LiquidSword:显卡算子不支持就必须反量化. 甚至CPU也是一样的, 如果CPU指令集不支持低精度数据进行运算, 那就要么反量化, 要么用纯软方法来模拟硬件运算(巨慢).   而且反量化还会有对齐/失真的等问题, 不是直接无脑反量化就能用的.//@LiquidSword:所以最后用的时候还要反量化到 8bit 吗

title:
立个FLAG, 今年下半年应该是多模态Agent之年. 

龙虾这类的Agent框架本身system prompt 巨大无比 + 多轮对话积累的 prompt + 图片/视频模态本身嵌入后 token
title_zh:
立个FLAG, 今年下半年应该是多模态Agent之年. 

龙虾这类的Agent框架本身system prompt 巨大无比 + 多轮对话积累的 prompt + 图片/视频模态本身嵌入后 token
summary_zh:
立个FLAG, 今年下半年应该是多模态Agent之年. 

龙虾这类的Agent框架本身system prompt 巨大无比 + 多轮对话积累的 prompt + 图片/视频模态本身嵌入后 token 量也不小, 所以目前最大的瓶颈反而又回到了 prefill 速度上, 即, 真正拼GPU性能的时代又回来了.

TFLOPS/TOPS 性能不够的情况下，多模态Agent ​​​

两张卡的差距主要在GPU性能上, B70

summary_zh: 来点垃圾佬冷知识，Dell PowerConnect 5524 交换机当年还做了个神操作, 用HDMI线当交换机堆叠线... P.S. 这是个全千兆交换机, 可见HDMI线带宽相当可以, 实际上 HDMI 1.0 就支持 4.9 Gbps 的带宽, 比用右边的2个SFP+(10Gbps)划算得多, 毕竟那个年代SFP+模块还是挺贵的. http://t.cn/AXf5q3MX //

title:
小米交作业了! 一口气放出两个新模型!

前几天放出的匿名模型 Hunter Alpha, 和 Healer Alpha 终于官宣了! Hunter Alpha 是小米 MiMo-V2-Pro, 1M
title_zh:
小米交作业了! 一口气放出两个新模型!

前几天放出的匿名模型 Hunter Alpha, 和 Healer Alpha 终于官宣了! Hunter Alpha 是小米 MiMo-V2-Pro, 1M
summary_zh:
小米交作业了! 一口气放出两个新模型!

前几天放出的匿名模型 Hunter Alpha, 和 Healer Alpha 终于官宣了! Hunter Alpha 是小米 MiMo-V2-Pro, 1M上下文, 1T 参数量 42B 激活参数, 支持多模态输入, 输出文本. 

Healer Alpha 是小米 MiMo-V2-Omni, 262K上下文, 支持文本, 音频, 图片, 视频输入.

title_zh: 回复@xiaojay:搭配其它 OCR skill, 视频里我魔改了paddleocr 官方那个skill, 官方那个默认是PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 (结构化OCR) , 这个识别游戏截图效果
summary_zh: 回复@xiaojay:搭配其它 OCR skill, 视频里我魔改了paddleocr 官方那个skill, 官方那个默认是PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 (结构化OCR) , 这个识别游戏截图效果不好, 换PP-OCRv5(纯OCR)就行了. 其他的纯OCR也可以, 尽量不要用结构化OCR, 识别游戏效果都一般. VLM 也行就是略贵了, 能白嫖尽量白嫖.//@xiaojay:glm tu

title: 回复@帅呆呆:第一个key如果有问题试试这个： openclaw config set agents.defaults.contextTokens 120000 --strict-json  将上下文
title_zh: 回复@帅呆呆:第一个key如果有问题试试这个： openclaw config set agents.defaults.contextTokens 120000 --strict-json  将上下文
summary_zh: 回复@帅呆呆:第一个key如果有问题试试这个： openclaw config set agents.defaults.contextTokens 120000 --strict-json  将上下文限制到120K，或者按照自己需求调整。//@帅呆呆:最后的命令里，234 都可以执行，第一条会报config validation failed: agents: unrecognized key:
